title: The Specialist pathway
metaTitle: Specialist pathway for overseas-trained specialists (SIMGs) in Australia
metaDescription: The Specialist pathway for overseas-trained specialists in Australia: how the relevant college assesses comparability, the area-of-need and short-term training options, and how it leads to specialist registration.
summaryMarkdown: The Specialist pathway is for specialist international medical graduates (SIMGs) — doctors with an overseas specialist qualification seeking specialist registration in Australia. [[cite:sp-who]] The defining step is assessment by the **relevant Australian specialist college**, which decides how comparable your training and experience are to the Australian-trained specialist. [[cite:sp-college]] If your qualification is on the Board's accepted list, check the faster [Expedited Specialist pathway](/pathways/expedited-specialist) first.

sections.heading: Specialist recognition
sections.bodyMarkdown: The main route is *specialist recognition*. You apply to the AMC for primary source verification of your qualifications, then the relevant college assesses comparability and rates you as **substantially comparable**, **partially comparable**, or **not comparable**. [[cite:sp-comparability]] Depending on the outcome you may take limited or provisional registration and complete a period of supervised practice and any college-required upskilling or exams, before applying for specialist registration. [[cite:sp-process]] Each [college](/colleges) sets its own process and fees.

sections.heading: Area of need
sections.bodyMarkdown: The *area of need* stream is for overseas-trained specialists filling a specialist-level position designated as an area of need. [[cite:sp-aon]] It's tied to a specific role, and is often combined with employer sponsorship.

sections.heading: Short-term training
sections.bodyMarkdown: There's also a *short-term training in a medical specialty* pathway, for specialists coming to Australia for a defined period of training or experience rather than long-term registration. [[cite:sp-stt]]

sections.heading: Is the expedited pathway faster for me?
sections.bodyMarkdown: For some specialties (general practice, anaesthesia, obstetrics and gynaecology, psychiatry, and from January 2026 general medicine and general paediatrics), eligible specialists can skip the comparability assessment entirely via the [Expedited Specialist pathway](/pathways/expedited-specialist). [[cite:sp-expedited]] If your specific qualification is on the accepted list, that route is usually faster.

sections.heading: English, visas and registration
sections.bodyMarkdown: You must meet the English language requirement [[cite:sp-english]] (see [English requirements](/english)), and most roles are employer-sponsored — see [visas](/visas). The pathway leads to specialist registration with the Medical Board. As an IMG you may also be affected by the Medicare moratorium — see [Medicare](/medicare).

claims.text: The Specialist pathway is for IMGs with an overseas specialist qualification seeking specialist registration.
The relevant Australian specialist college assesses how comparable the applicant's training is.
Colleges rate overseas specialists as substantially comparable, partially comparable, or not comparable.
Applicants apply to the AMC for primary source verification, take limited/provisional registration for supervised practice, then apply for specialist registration once college requirements are met.
The area-of-need stream is for overseas specialists filling a designated area-of-need specialist position.
A short-term training in a medical specialty pathway exists for defined periods of training or experience.
Eligible specialists in listed specialties can skip the comparability assessment via the Expedited Specialist pathway.
Applicants must meet the Medical Board's English language skills registration standard.

faq.question: What does 'substantially comparable' mean?
faq.answerMarkdown: It's the most favourable comparability rating — your training is judged closely comparable to the Australian-trained specialist, usually meaning a shorter period of supervised practice before specialist registration.

faq.question: Which college assesses me?
faq.answerMarkdown: The Australian specialist college for your specialty (for example RACP, RACS, RACGP, ACEM or RANZCP). See the [colleges](/colleges) directory.

faq.question: Should I use the specialist or expedited pathway?
faq.answerMarkdown: If your specific qualification is on the Board's accepted-qualifications list for an eligible specialty, the expedited pathway skips the comparability assessment and is usually faster. Otherwise, the standard specialist pathway applies.
